title: Chakbhabani Kalibari
tagline: Chakbhabani Kalibari — spiritual town of West Bengal

overview.short: Chakbhabani Kalibari is an historic temple at Balurghat in the West Bengal State of India.
overview.description: Chakbhabani Kalibari is an historic temple at Balurghat in the West Bengal State of India. The temple is close to Balurghat Girl's High School, Balurghat Jail, Administrative Office, Municipal Office and Balurghat College.

topPlaces.name: Chak Bhrigu
topPlaces.description: Chakvrigu is a census town in Balurghat CD Block in Balurghat subdivision of Dakshin Dinajpur district in the state of West Bengal, India.

topPlaces.name: Dakra
topPlaces.description: Dakra is a census town in Balurghat CD Block in Balurghat subdivision of Dakshin Dinajpur district in the state of West Bengal, India.

topPlaces.name: Balurghat Mahila Mahavidyalaya
topPlaces.description: Balurghat Mahila Mahavidyalaya is a women's college in Balurghat in the Dakshin Dinajpur district of West Bengal, India. The college is affiliated to University of Gour Banga, offering undergraduate courses.

topPlaces.name: Balurghat (community development block)
topPlaces.description: Balurghat is a community development block that forms an administrative division in Balurghat subdivision of Dakshin Dinajpur district in the Indian state of West Bengal.

topPlaces.name: Balurghat
topPlaces.description: Balurghat is a city and municipality in the Dakshin Dinajpur district of West Bengal, India, and serves as the district headquarters. Located along National Highway 512 and the Atrai River, it is an important center for trade, education, and administration.

topPlaces.name: Atrai River
topPlaces.description: Atrai River (also spelt as Atreyee) (Bengali: আত্রাই/আত্রেয়ী নদী, romanized: ātrāi/ātrēẏī nadī) flows in West Bengal in India and northern parts of Bangladesh. In ancient times the river was called Atreyee and finds a mention in the Mahabharata, one of the two Sanskrit epics of ancient India.

topPlaces.name: Jawahar Navodaya Vidyalaya, Dakshin Dinajpur
topPlaces.description: Jawahar Navodaya Vidyalaya, Dakshin Dinajpur is a boarding, co-educational school in Dakshin Dinajpur district of West Bengal in India. Navodaya Vidyalayas are funded by the Indian Ministry of Human Resources Development and administered by Navodaya Vidyalaya Smiti, an autonomous body under the MHRD ministry.

topPlaces.name: Bara Kashipur
topPlaces.description: Bara Kashipur (also called Barakashipur) is the small village located in Balurghat of the Dakshin Dinajpur district of West Bengal in India. Hili (Bangladesh border) is situated few kilometers from the heart of the village.

items.note: Chakvrigu is a census town in Balurghat CD Block in Balurghat subdivision of Dakshin Dinajpur district in the state of West Bengal, India.
Dakra is a census town in Balurghat CD Block in Balurghat subdivision of Dakshin Dinajpur district in the state of West Bengal, India.

Balurghat Mahila Mahavidyalaya is a women's college in Balurghat in the Dakshin Dinajpur district of West Bengal, India. The college is affiliated to University of Gour Banga, offering undergraduate courses.
Balurghat is a community development block that forms an administrative division in Balurghat subdivision of Dakshin Dinajpur district in the Indian state of West Bengal.

Balurghat is a city and municipality in the Dakshin Dinajpur district of West Bengal, India, and serves as the district headquarters. Located along National Highway 512 and the Atrai River, it is an important center for trade, education, and administration.

Atrai River (also spelt as Atreyee) (Bengali: আত্রাই/আত্রেয়ী নদী, romanized: ātrāi/ātrēẏī nadī) flows in West Bengal in India and northern parts of Bangladesh. In ancient times the river was called Atreyee and finds a mention in the Mahabharata, one of the two Sanskrit epics of ancient India.

itinerary.title: Day 3 — Jawahar Navodaya Vidyalaya, Dakshin Dinajpur & around

items.note: Jawahar Navodaya Vidyalaya, Dakshin Dinajpur is a boarding, co-educational school in Dakshin Dinajpur district of West Bengal in India. Navodaya Vidyalayas are funded by the Indian Ministry of Human Resources Development and administered by Navodaya Vidyalaya Smiti, an autonomous body under the MHRD ministry.

Bara Kashipur (also called Barakashipur) is the small village located in Balurghat of the Dakshin Dinajpur district of West Bengal in India. Hili (Bangladesh border) is situated few kilometers from the heart of the village.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Chakbhabani Kalibari?
faq.a: The best time to visit Chakbhabani Kalibari is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Chakbhabani Kalibari?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Bagdogra Airport (Siliguri/Darjeeling) (167 km) and the nearest railway station is Katihar Junction (125 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Chakbhabani Kalibari?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Chakbhabani Kalibari at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Chakbhabani Kalibari?
faq.a: Stays in Chakbhabani Kalibari start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Chakbhabani Kalibari?
faq.a: The top attractions are Chak Bhrigu, Dakra, Balurghat Mahila Mahavidyalaya, Balurghat (community development block), Balurghat.
